title: Dog Fly Bites on Ears (Fly Strike): Triage and Care Guide
meta_desc: Learn about fly strike on dog ear tips. Urgency is low, but veterinary care helps prevent infection. See why photos are vital for triage.
content_html:
What Are Fly Bites on Dog Ears?

Fly strike occurs when biting flies, such as stable flies, are attracted to the delicate skin on a dog's ear tips. These flies bite the skin to feed on blood, resulting in small, painful, and often bloody sores. You may notice black or dark brown crusts, bleeding, and hair loss at the very edges of the ears. This condition is most common in outdoor dogs or during warmer months when fly populations are high.

Is This an Emergency?

The urgency level for fly bites is Low. While the condition is irritating and can lead to secondary infections or permanent scarring if left untreated, it is rarely a life-threatening emergency. However, if your dog is lethargic, the ears are swelling significantly, or you see maggots (myiasis) in the wounds, you should contact a veterinarian more promptly.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Providing a high-quality photo of your dog's ears is incredibly helpful for veterinary triage. Clear images allow a professional to distinguish between simple fly strike and other conditions that look similar, such as ear margin vasculitis (an immune-mediated issue), sarcoptic mange (mites), or fungal infections. Seeing the exact pattern of the scabbing helps determine if over-the-counter ointments are sufficient or if prescription medications are required.

faqs.question: How can I prevent flies from biting my dog's ears?
faqs.content: Keeping your dog indoors during the heat of the day, using pet-safe fly repellents, and applying a thick layer of petroleum jelly or vet-approved ointment to the ear tips can create a physical barrier against flies.

faqs.question: Can I use human fly spray on my dog?
faqs.content: No, you should never use human insect repellents containing DEET or other chemicals on dogs, as they can be toxic if licked or absorbed. Always use products specifically labeled for canine use.

faqs.question: Will the hair on the ear tips grow back?
faqs.content: Yes, in most cases, once the flies are deterred and the skin is allowed to heal, the hair will eventually grow back. However, chronic fly strike can cause minor permanent scarring or thickening of the ear leather.
